title: Australia, UK tackle AI safety
full_text:
Australia and Britain have signed a formal agreement to share information on artificial intelligence risks – before Australia’s own AI safety watchdog even exists.
The Memorandum of Understanding, signed on Sunday between the Australian AI Safety Institute and the UK AI Security Institute, commits both governments to joint research, shared testing methods and co-ordinated responses to emerging AI threats. But the Australian institute is still being established.
Canberra is committing to international AI safety obligations under the federal government’s National AI Plan while the domestic apparatus to honour them remains on paper.
Under the MoU, Australia and Britain will share information on emerging AI capabilities and risks, conduct joint research on how to measure and manage potential threats, and support the International Network for Advanced AI Measurement, Evaluation and Science – a multilateral body of which both countries are founding members. The agreement was signed during a visit to Australia by UK Minister for AI and Online Safety Kanishka Narayan and Australia’s Assistant Minister for Science, Technology and the Digital Economy, Andrew Charlton.
